 -g, --get                       Download exploit files

local_help.man:
pompem(1)               Exploit and Vulnerability Finder              pompem(1)

NAME
     pompem - Exploit and Vulnerability Finder

SYNOPSIS
     pompem [OPTIONS] <keyword>

DESCRIPTION
     Find  exploit  with  a system of advanced search, designed to automate the
     search for Exploits and Vulnerability in the most important databases  fa-
     cilitating  the  work of pentesters, ethical hackers and forensics expert.
     Performs searches in databases: PacketStorm security, CXSecurity, ZeroDay,
     Vulners, National Vulnerability Database, WPScan  Vulnerability  Database.
     This tool is essential in the security of networks and systems.

     The search results can be exported to HTML or text format.

OPTIONS
     -s | --search <keyword>
            Search by <keyword>

     --txt  Write txt file

     --html
            Write html file

     --update
            Upgrade to latest version

     -g | --get
            Download exploit files

     -h | --help
            Get basic options and Help

AUTHOR
     The POMPEM was written by Rafael Francischini <rafinha.unix@gmail.com> and
     Bruno Fraga <officer@brunofraga.net>

     This   manual   page   was  written  by  Giovani  Augusto  Ferreira  <gio-
     vani@riseup.net> for the Debian project (but may be used by others).

POMPEM 0.2.0                        Ago 2016                          pompem(1)

apt_metadata.description:
Exploit and Vulnerability Finder
Find exploit with a system of advanced search, designed to automate the search
for Exploits and Vulnerability in the most important databases facilitating
the work of pentesters, ethical hackers and forensics expert. Performs searches
in databases: PacketStorm security, CXSecurity, ZeroDay, Vulners, National
Vulnerability Database, WPScan Vulnerability Database. This tool is essential
in the security of networks and systems.
.
The search results can be exported to HTML or text format.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use pompem because pompem - Exploit and Vulnerability Finder.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pompem [OPTIONS] <keyword>` as documented upstream for pompem.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair pompem with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `pompem --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install pompem. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `pompem [OPTIONS] <keyword>`. pompem - Exploit and Vulnerability Finder.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: pompem - Exploit and Vulnerability Finder.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: pompem - Exploit and Vulnerability Finder.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install pompem
Install with: sudo apt install pompem
